description_long: This object consists of three pillar candles of varying heights and colors—lavender, white, and red—arranged closely together on a round, flat, brown base that resembles a wooden or ceramic plate. The candles have irregular, melted tops and visible wax drips running down their sides, suggesting they have been partially burned. The surface of the candles is matte and slightly rough, with subtle textural details that mimic real wax. The base is smooth and solid, providing stable support for the candles. No flames or wicks are visible on any of the candles. The overall appearance is decorative and suitable for placement on a table or shelf in an urban environment.
description: Three partially melted pillar candles in lavender, white, and red sit on a round brown base, with visible wax drips and a realistic waxy texture.
description_view_0: Front view shows all three candles clearly, with the lavender candle at the back, white on the left, and red on the right, all on a round brown base.
description_view_1: This view centers the candles, highlighting the lavender and red candles at the back and the white candle in front, with the brown base fully visible.
description_view_2: From this angle, the red candle is most prominent, with the lavender candle behind and the white candle to the left, all on the round base.
description_view_3: The lavender and red candles are most visible, with the white candle partially obscured, and the brown base is seen from a slight angle.
